Okay, so I'm going to tell you about a little mistake and a solution that you'll want to use and incorporate on a daily basis when you hear price as an issue. Salespeople often make the mistake and managers too of always offering something for a lower price whenever faced with a price objection as a way to resolve what appears to be the money problem. This is incorrect. It's a solution based on false belief that price is the only reason people don't buy things. Okay, well let me bring him down to something less. Don't do that. When you move the customer down in price to offer something cheaper, they're actually less likely to even want the next product if they didn't want the first one. This will cause your buyer to actually believe that you don't have a solution and that he or she is just wasting their time. One of the other ways to discover other concerns is to actually move the buyer up when a price objection comes up rather than down. This will get your customer thinking in terms of value, not price, and will determine whether or not the price objection is even valid or not. Look, if he believes that his girl will love the gift and really wants to make her happy, showing him something more expensive will actually get you closer to the sale on the product that he objected to because of the price.
